description: HTTP Request built-in pagination with cursor/starting_after style — not a hand-rolled Loop Over Items loop.

conversation.text: On manual trigger, fetch all customers from GET https://api.stripe-style.example.com/v1/customers and return the full list. The API uses cursor paging — keep going until there is nothing left.

outcomeExpectations: The HTTP Request node fetches across all cursor pages via built-in pagination, not just the first page.
The agent did NOT work around paging with a Loop Over Items node, a Code node loop, or a hard-coded number of requests.

executionScenarios.name: cursor-two-pages
executionScenarios.description: First page has has_more true; second page completes the list
executionScenarios.dataSetup: This is a CURSOR-PAGINATED endpoint. `GET https://api.stripe-style.example.com/v1/customers` with no starting_after returns { "data": [{ "id": "cus_001", "name": "Acme" }, { "id": "cus_002", "name": "Beta Co" }], "has_more": true }. `GET ...?starting_after=cus_002` (or equivalent cursor from the last item of page 1) returns { "data": [{ "id": "cus_003", "name": "Gamma LLC" }], "has_more": false }. Any request with starting_after=cus_003 or beyond returns { "data": [], "has_more": false }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors and returns all THREE customers — Acme, Beta Co, and Gamma LLC — having followed the cursor through both pages. A workflow that stops after the first page (only Acme and Beta Co) FAILS.
